## What You Will Learn

In PyTorch, every neural network is defined as a **class inheriting from nn.Module**. This is the standard pattern used across all deep learning projects, from simple classifiers to GPT-4.

### Key Concepts
- **nn.Module**: The base class for all neural network modules. Provides parameter tracking, GPU transfer, and state dict functionality.
- **__init__()**: Defines your layers (Linear, Conv2d, etc.) as instance variables.
- **forward()**: Defines how data flows through your network — this is your computation graph.
- **nn.Linear**: A fully connected (dense) layer that applies a linear transformation: y = xW^T + b
- **nn.ReLU**: The Rectified Linear Unit activation: f(x) = max(0, x). Introduces non-linearity.

### Why This Matters
This nn.Module pattern is universal. Every PyTorch model you ever see — ResNet, Transformer, BERT, Stable Diffusion — follows this exact structure.
